ei.headline: High-return engagement period
ei.context_note: Ei day: "favorable for entering office and receiving appointment"
ei.detail: Your input visibly improves the site, and deliverables exceed client expectations. This is the time to land high-value contracts. Propose boldly. Price confidently.
do: Propose improvements beyond contract scope
Use your first deliverable to build trust, then pitch add-ons immediately
Price to reflect your actual value; do not discount
avoid: Doing the bare minimum within the contract

shin.headline: Trust-building period
shin.context_note: Shin day: "favorable for making connections and settling commitments"
shin.detail: The site manager has a good impression of you and is willing to provide resources and latitude. Good time to sign long-term or framework agreements. The client will proactively refer you to other departments or group companies.
do: Build a direct trust relationship with the site manager
Negotiate framework or annual contracts
Ask for referrals to other projects within the client organization
avoid: Communicating only through the liaison without building decision-maker relationships
Having a strong relationship but failing to extend it

yu.headline: Network monetization period
yu.context_note: Yu day: "favorable for making friends, greatly auspicious"
yu.detail: Your professional network is your biggest pipeline right now. Attend industry events and tech communities. Opportunities surface through relationships. Take on projects that require multi-stakeholder coordination.
do: Attend industry events to build pipeline
Take on projects requiring cross-departmental coordination
Leverage existing client referrals
avoid: Over-investing in networking without converting to contracts

sui.headline: Technical recharge period
sui.context_note: Sui day: "suited only for resolving troubles and healing"
sui.detail: Not the right time for new engagements. Invest in upskilling, certifications, and documenting past project methodologies. This investment becomes your pricing leverage in the next cycle.
do: Pursue professional certifications
Document past projects into methodology papers
Wrap up existing engagement deliverables cleanly

an.headline: Foundation-building period
an.context_note: An day: "favorable for relocation, building gardens and homes"
an.detail: Good time to join a new site or change your resident assignment. Your stabilizing ability is what the site needs, especially teams in startup or transition phases. Focus on building processes and systems after you join.
do: Join sites building new teams
Prioritize SOP and workflow setup after joining
Help the site establish foundational infrastructure
avoid: Only firefighting without building lasting systems

kai.headline: Deadlock-breaking period
kai.context_note: Kai day: "suited for suppression and subduing adversaries; all else is unbearable"
kai.detail: You deliver the most value when breaking through impasses. Pick sites with gnarly technical debt or organizational gridlock. Go in, solve the core problem, get out. Do not take on routine operations work.
do: Pick sites with clear pain points
Focus on the core issue after joining; deliver quickly
Exit after delivery; do not get pulled into operations
avoid: Absorbing miscellaneous tasks beyond the core mission

ki.headline: Internal relationship period
ki.context_note: Ki day: "favorable for socializing and gatherings" + "unfavorable for distant travel and trade"
ki.detail: Channel your interpersonal skills into strengthening existing client relationships. Lunch with the site team, understand their real needs. Not the time for new business development or big deal negotiations.
do: Deepen existing client relationships
Build informal communication channels with the site team
Uncover latent needs for your next proposal

sei.headline: Delivery and achievement period
sei.context_note: Sei day: "favorable for studying and achieving results, all auspicious"
sei.detail: Learning and delivery are both in your favor. New skills translate directly into site output. Take on projects with clear acceptance criteria. You will hit deadlines or beat them.
do: Take projects with clear milestones
Learn new skills and apply them to the site immediately
Show delivery results with data
avoid: Taking projects with vague acceptance criteria

mei.headline: Preparation and positioning period
mei.context_note: Mei day: "unfavorable for taking action on all matters"
mei.detail: Not the time to push. Spend this period reviewing your consulting methodology, updating your portfolio, and reassessing your market positioning. You will be better prepared when the next good opportunity appears.
do: Review and refine your consulting methodology
Update your portfolio and case studies
Reassess your market positioning and rate
avoid: Filling your calendar out of anxiety

gyo.headline: Energy accumulation period
gyo.context_note: Gyo day: "actions yield no results, greatly unfavorable"
gyo.detail: Everything feels like running against the wind right now. Channel your energy into industry trend research and learning new frameworks. Build ammunition for the next business cycle. Do not chase revenue out of anxiety.
do: Research industry trends
Learn new technical frameworks
Organize past engagements into a knowledge base

tai.headline: Guided learning period
tai.context_note: Tai day: "unfavorable for taking action on all matters"
tai.detail: Join a site with mature methodology and learn from their existing systems. You are the potential type. The hands-on experience you accumulate now becomes the capital for independent engagements later.
do: Join large-scale sites with mature systems
Pair with a senior consultant to learn
Accumulate hands-on experience within existing frameworks
avoid: Forcing yourself to prove independent value too early
